conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I'm planning the architecture for a real-time chat feature. I'm debating between WebSockets, Server-Sent Events, and long polling.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Long polling is the simplest to implement but has the worst performance characteristics.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: SSE works well for one-way streams but chat is bidirectional.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: WebSockets handle bidirectional communication natively and our infra already supports it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Decision made: WebSockets. The bidirectional requirement rules out SSE, and long polling is a non-starter for latency reasons.

query: What technology did the user choose to implement their real-time chat feature?
